acceptance_criteria: Floor violations propagate as structured errors.
`Get-EffectiveConfig` refuses to return a config that violates the floor.
description: After merging, validate that the effective configuration does not violate the hard safety floor: remote script execution, privilege elevation, encoded commands, broad protected-root deletion, cloud mutation in `/x`, and package install in `/x` cannot be enabled. Project config can only relax `protectedRoots` when user policy sets `allowProjectPolicyRelaxation = true`.
notes: This task is the last line of defence ŌĆö keep the rule list tight and append-only.
Document which rules are floor (immutable) vs configurable in `docs/SAFETY.md` (doc-01).
steps: TDD: Effective config with `policy.blockedBehavior.blockRemoteScriptExecution = false` is rejected.
TDD: Project config attempting to remove `~/.ssh` from `protectedRoots` is rejected when user policy disallows relaxation.
TDD: Project config can add safe write roots inside the project (per ┬¦9.7).
TDD: Project config can add stricter regex patterns (per ┬¦9.7).
TDD: Floor violation surfaces as a structured error, not a silent override.
Wire it into `Get-EffectiveConfig` post-merge ŌĆö failure prevents callers from using the merged config.
name: [cfg-11] Enforce safety policy floor against project / CLI overrides
